summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that unauthorized introspection queries succeed in protect-all mode, exposing sensitive schema fields like adminSecret and deleteUser that should require authorization.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that an introspection query from an unauthenticated user (resolveUserFn returns null) exposes the complete schema including sensitive fields.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, executed the introspection query without authentication, and confirmed that sensitive fields (adminSecret, deleteUser) were exposed in the schema response.
verification_method: The exploit script checks: (1) unauthenticated user (resolveUserFn returns null), (2) introspection query succeeds, (3) sensitive field names (adminSecret, deleteUser) are visible in __schema response. This confirms authorization bypass for introspection.
evidence_produced_by_run: Query type fields include adminSecret and deleteUser
protect-all mode did not block introspection fields
Authorization check bypassed for isIntrospectionType fields

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and builds the Docker image.
